inputs.label: Birth Year
inputs.businessContext: Your birth year determines your full retirement age (FRA). Enter year as integer (e.g., 1965).

inputs.label: Early Retirement Age
inputs.businessContext: The age at which you plan to start receiving benefits (minimum 62, maximum 70). Use decimal for months (e.g., 62 and 6 months = 62.5).

inputs.label: Monthly Benefit at Full Retirement Age
inputs.businessContext: The monthly benefit you would receive if you wait until full retirement age (primary insurance amount).

inputs.label: Cost-of-Living Adjustment Factor
inputs.businessContext: Multiplier to adjust for COLA (1 = no adjustment). Usually 1 for current benefit calculations.

fullRetirementAge.description: Determine FRA based on birth year according to Social Security rules.

monthsEarly.description: Number of months before FRA that benefits are claimed.

reductionFactor.description: Reduction factor applied to PIA for early claiming.

reducedMonthlyBenefit.description: Monthly benefit at early retirement age, adjusted by COLA scaling.
